name: Razorpay SRE — SLO Monitoring & Alerting Strategy
scenario: Razorpay wants to implement SLO-based reliability engineering. Build an error budget tracker, define burn rate alerts, write runbooks, and configure alertmanager routing so the on-call team gets actionable, well-routed alerts.

core.description: SLOConfig: define SLO=99.9%, error budget=0.1%, error budget in minutes=43.2. As constants.
ErrorBudgetTracker @Service: Counter for success/failure, Gauge for budget.remaining.percent and budget.consumed.minutes. Methods: record(), budgetRemaining(), shouldFreezeDeployments().
HeartbeatService @Service: @Scheduled(30s) heartbeat counter. prometheus alert rule: absent() for 3 minutes.
prometheus/rules.yml: 4 rules — PaymentErrorHigh (1%), PaymentP99High (500ms), PaymentFastBurn (14.4×), PaymentServiceDown (absent). All have for: duration and runbook_url.
@SpringBootTest (4 tests): full budget healthy, exhausted budget freezes deploys, heartbeat increments, error budget gauge registers. All 4 pass.
README: explains SLI/SLO/SLA distinction + what each alert rule does.

bonus.description: alertmanager.yml: routing (critical→PagerDuty, warning→Slack) + inhibition (ServiceDown suppresses ErrorHigh + P99High).
SlowBurnAlert: 6× burn rate on 6h+30m windows. Separate @SpringBootTest test verifying slow burn detection.
ErrorBudgetDashboard: runbook as a Java constant (multiline string), all 5 anti-patterns documented with fixes.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. Error budget = 1 - SLO (0.001 for 99.9% SLO). budgetRemaining% = (1 - currentErrorRate/errorBudget) × 100. Fast burn multiplier 14.4 means: at this rate, the full 30-day budget is exhausted in 30/14.4 ≈ 2 days. absent() fires when the metric has NO data points — the service stopped sending metrics. for: 5m in alert rules means the condition must be true continuously for 5 minutes before the alert fires — prevents alerting on momentary spikes.
